_note: estimated — run `go test -bench=. -benchmem -benchtime=3s ./...` for real numbers on your hardware

results.note: Pure memory allocation + mutex. No I/O. This is the ceiling without disk.

results.description: Disk-backed produce throughput with batch fsync (1KB messages, fsync every 64 writes)
results.note: batch fsync every 64 writes. Without batching (fsync-per-write) this would be ~3,200 msg/sec.

results.description: Sequential consume throughput from disk (256-byte messages, 100-message batches)
results.note: Sequential scan at near-memory speed due to OS page cache warming after first read.

results.description: Offset seek time via sparse index (binary search, 1000 messages preloaded)
results.note: Binary search on index file + short linear scan. At 1M messages this extrapolates to ~0.2ms.

results.description: Consumer group offset commit latency (writes to __consumer_offsets topic)
results.note: Dominated by disk write to __consumer_offsets. Batching commits (like auto.commit.interval.ms) reduces this cost.
